description_long: This object is a small, fixed-wing airplane with a straight, low-mounted wing configuration and a single vertical stabilizer at the tail. The fuselage appears compact, with a forward-mounted cockpit that is likely enclosed, though window details are indistinct. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the fuselage, with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral angles. The landing gear is fixed and tricycle-type, with two main gear units under the wings and a nose gear visible. The surface is primarily a dull gray with some markings and minor color accents, suggesting a utilitarian or training aircraft. The overall structure is metallic, with some plastic or composite elements likely present in the landing gear and cockpit area.
description: A small, gray, fixed-wing airplane with a low-mounted straight wing, single vertical stabilizer, and fixed tricycle landing gear. The cockpit is forward-mounted and the surface shows utilitarian markings.
description_view_0: From this angle, the airplane's nose, forward cockpit, and main wing structure are clearly visible, with the landing gear and tail section partially obscured.
description_view_1: This view highlights the rear of the airplane, showing the single vertical stabilizer and the tail section more prominently, with the wings extending outward.
description_view_2: The left side of the airplane is visible here, with the wing and landing gear in clear view, and the cockpit area slightly more obscured.
description_view_3: This angle provides a clear look at the right side of the airplane, with the nose, cockpit, and wing structure visible, as well as the fixed landing gear.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, straight configuration with no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed tricycle landing gear with two main gear units and a nose gear
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, likely enclosed cockpit with single-seat configuration
